L2.description: A cryptographic weakness exists in randomly-generated alphanumeric string utility functions where the output always contains at least one numeric digit. When no digit is naturally present in the generated string, one is forcibly inserted at a random position, replacing an existing character. This guarantees the presence of a digit in every output, significantly reducing the entropy of short strings and making them more predictable than expected.

L3.area: Random alphanumeric string generation utilities, including both standard and cryptographically-secure variants
L3.description: A cryptographic weakness exists in alphanumeric random string generation where both the standard pseudo-random and cryptographically-secure variants enforce that the output always contains at least one numeric digit. When a generated string lacks a digit, one is forcibly inserted at a random position, replacing an existing character. This reduces the effective entropy of short strings, making them more predictable than expected for security-sensitive use cases.

ground_truth.reason: The RandomAlphaNumeric and CryptoRandomAlphaNumeric functions forcibly insert a numeric digit when one is not naturally present in the generated string, significantly reducing the entropy of short randomly-generated alphanumeric strings.
